abandoned.en: left alone and no longer wanted or cared for

ex.en: The old house has been abandoned for years.
ex.ko: 그 오래된 집은 수년간 버려져 있었다.

abnormally.en: in a way that is different from what is normal or usual

ex.en: The river levels are abnormally high this year.

abolish.en: officially end a law, system, or institution

ex.en: The government abolished the old tax law.

aboriginal.en: relating to the people who were the original inhabitants of a region

ex.en: The museum displays aboriginal art from Australia.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 호주 원주민 예술품을 전시한다.

ex.en: The car stopped abruptly.

absentee.en: a person who is not present when expected to be

ex.en: The teacher marked the absentees on the list.
It's absurd to expect me to finish this in an hour.
ex.ko: 이걸 한 시간 안에 끝내라는 건 터무니없다.

abuse.en: cruel or violent treatment of a person or animal

ex.en: The organization protects children from abuse.

abuse.en: improper or excessive use of something

ex.en: Abuse of power is a serious problem in politics.

abuse.en: treat someone with cruelty or violence

ex.en: He was arrested for abusing his employees.

accessibility.cue: 쉽게 다가가거나 이용할 수 있는 정도
accessibility.en: the quality of being easy to reach, enter, or use

ex.en: The new building was designed with accessibility in mind.
ex.ko: 그 새 건물은 접근성을 고려해 설계되었다.

accommodation.en: a place where someone can live or stay, especially while traveling

ex.en: The hotel offers comfortable accommodation for tourists.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 여행객들에게 편안한 숙소를 제공한다.

accordingly.en: in a way that is suitable for a particular situation, or as a result of it

ex.en: The rules changed, so we adjusted our plans accordingly.
ex.ko: 규칙이 바뀌어서 우리는 그에 따라 계획을 조정했다.

accumulate.en: gradually gather or increase in quantity over time

ex.en: Dust had accumulated on the shelves.

accusation.en: a claim that someone has done something wrong or illegal

ex.en: She denied the accusation of theft.
Set goals that are realistic and achievable.

acid.en: a chemical substance that can dissolve metal and often has a sour taste

ex.en: The acid in lemons gives them a sour taste.
He made an acid remark about her work.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 작업에 대해 신랄한 말을 했다.

acquisition.en: the process of learning or acquiring a skill or knowledge

ex.en: Language acquisition happens naturally in children.

acquisition.en: the act of buying or obtaining something, such as a company or property

ex.en: The acquisition of the smaller company doubled their profits.
ex.ko: 그 작은 회사의 인수로 이익이 두 배가 되었다.

actively.en: in an energetic or enthusiastic way, taking part in something

ex.en: She is actively involved in local politics.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지역 정치에 적극적으로 참여하고 있다.

ex.en: The actual cost was higher than we expected.
ex.ko: 실제 비용은 우리가 예상한 것보다 높았다.

acute.en: (of an illness) coming on suddenly and severely

ex.en: He was taken to the hospital with acute appendicitis.
There is an acute shortage of clean water in the region.
ex.ko: 그 지역에는 깨끗한 물이 극심하게 부족하다.

acute.en: showing great ability to notice or understand things

ex.en: She has an acute sense of hearing.

adaptable.en: able to change easily to suit new conditions

ex.en: Cats are highly adaptable animals.

addict.en: a person who cannot stop using a harmful substance, such as drugs

ex.en: He became a drug addict in his twenties.

addict.en: cause someone to become dependent on something

ex.en: Social media is designed to addict its users.

addicted.en: unable to stop using or doing something harmful

ex.en: He is addicted to caffeine.

addiction.en: a strong physical or mental need to do or have something

ex.en: She is receiving treatment for her addiction to alcohol.
ex.ko: 그녀는 알코올 중독 치료를 받고 있다.

additionally.en: in addition to what has already been mentioned

ex.en: Additionally, the hotel offers free breakfast.
ex.ko: 게다가 그 호텔은 무료 아침 식사를 제공한다.

adequate.en: enough or good enough for a particular purpose

ex.en: We need adequate time to prepare for the exam.
ex.ko: 우리는 시험을 준비할 충분한 시간이 필요하다.

adequately.en: in a way that is enough or good enough

ex.en: The workers were not adequately trained for the job.
ex.ko: 그 근로자들은 그 일에 충분히 훈련받지 못했다.

adjustment.en: a small change made to something to make it work better

ex.en: We made a few adjustments to the schedule.

adjustment.en: the process of becoming used to a new situation

ex.en: Moving abroad requires a period of adjustment.
ex.ko: 해외로 이주하는 것은 적응 기간이 필요하다.

administrative.en: relating to the management of a business or organization

ex.en: She handles all the administrative tasks in the office.
ex.ko: 그녀는 사무실의 모든 행정 업무를 처리한다.

ex.en: Her courage in the face of danger was admirable.
ex.ko: 위험 앞에서 보인 그녀의 용기는 존경할 만했다.

admiringly.en: in a way that shows admiration

ex.en: He looked at the painting admiringly.

admittedly.en: used to admit that something is true before making another point

ex.en: Admittedly, the plan has some risks.
ex.ko: 인정하건대, 그 계획에는 위험 요소가 좀 있다.

adopted.en: taken into a family as one's own child, though not related by birth

ex.en: She grew up as an adopted child.

adoption.en: the legal process of taking another person's child as your own

ex.en: They are planning to go through adoption.

adoption.en: the act of choosing to use a new idea, method, or plan

ex.en: The adoption of new technology increased productivity.

adore.en: love or admire someone or something very much

ex.en: She adores her little nephew.

adorn.en: make something more attractive by decorating it

ex.en: Flowers adorned the tables at the wedding.
Advancements in medicine have saved many lives.

advancement.en: a move to a higher position or rank at work

ex.en: He worked hard to earn a career advancement.

advent.en: the arrival or beginning of something important

ex.en: The advent of the internet changed how we communicate.
ex.ko: 인터넷의 도래는 우리의 소통 방식을 바꾸었다.

adventurous.en: willing to try new and exciting or risky things

ex.en: She has an adventurous spirit and loves to travel.

adverse.en: unfavorable or harmful to someone or something

ex.en: The flight was delayed due to adverse weather conditions.

adversely.en: in a way that has a negative or harmful effect

ex.en: The drought adversely affected crop production.
ex.ko: 가뭄은 농작물 생산에 부정적인 영향을 미쳤다.

ex.en: She showed great strength in the face of adversity.
ex.ko: 그녀는 역경 속에서도 큰 강인함을 보여주었다.

affectionate.en: showing warmth and love toward someone

ex.en: He gave his daughter an affectionate hug.

affectionately.en: in a way that shows love or warmth

ex.en: She smiled affectionately at the baby.
ex.ko: 그녀는 아기를 보며 다정하게 미소 지었다.

affordable.en: not too expensive; that people can afford

ex.en: The city needs more affordable housing.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 더 저렴한 주택이 필요하다.

ageless.en: never seeming to grow old or go out of style

ex.en: Her beauty seemed ageless.
ex.ko: 그녀의 아름다움은 나이를 초월한 듯 보였다.

aggressively.en: in a forceful or hostile way

ex.en: The dog barked aggressively at the stranger.
ex.ko: 그 개는 낯선 사람을 향해 공격적으로 짖었다.

agonize.en: think anxiously and for a long time about a difficult decision

ex.en: She agonized over whether to accept the job offer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 일자리 제안을 받아들일지 오래 고민했다.

ex.en: He cried out in agony after the injury.
ex.ko: 그는 부상 후 극심한 고통에 소리쳤다.

aircraft.en: a vehicle that can fly, such as an airplane or helicopter

ex.en: The aircraft landed safely despite the storm.

aircrew.en: the people who operate an aircraft, including pilots and cabin crew

ex.en: The aircrew prepared the plane for takeoff.
ex.ko: 항공 승무원들은 이륙을 위해 비행기를 준비했다.

alcoholism.en: a medical condition of being unable to control one's drinking of alcohol

ex.en: He sought help for his alcoholism.
ex.ko: 그는 알코올 중독 치료를 위해 도움을 구했다.

alert.en: warn someone about a possible danger or problem

ex.en: The security guard alerted the police about the break-in.

alert.en: quick to notice and react to things

ex.en: Stay alert while driving at night.
ex.ko: 밤에 운전할 때는 정신을 바짝 차려라.

alert.en: a warning of danger or a problem

ex.en: A fire alert went off in the building.

allegation.en: a claim that someone has done something wrong, without proof yet

ex.en: The company denied the allegations of fraud.

alliance.en: a formal agreement between countries or groups to work together

ex.en: The two countries formed a military alliance.

ally.en: a country or person that helps and supports another

ex.en: The United States and the United Kingdom are close allies.

ally.en: join with someone or something for a shared purpose

ex.en: The two companies allied to develop new technology.
ex.ko: 두 회사는 신기술 개발을 위해 협력했다.

ex.en: The boat pulled up alongside the dock.
ex.ko: 그 배는 부두 옆에 나란히 정박했다.

alphabetical.en: arranged according to the order of the letters of the alphabet

ex.en: The names are listed in alphabetical order.

altar.en: a raised table or structure used for religious ceremonies

ex.en: The couple exchanged vows at the altar.
They altered the plan at the last minute.

alternate.en: do or use two or more things by turns, one after the other

ex.en: We alternate cooking dinner between us.

alternate.en: used instead of the usual one

ex.en: Please provide an alternate route in case of traffic.
ex.ko: 교통 체증에 대비해 대체 경로를 알려주세요.

alternate.en: occurring one after the other in a repeated pattern

ex.en: She works on alternate weekends.

alternatively.en: used to introduce another possible option or choice

ex.en: You could take the bus, or alternatively, walk.
ex.ko: 버스를 타도 되고, 아니면 걸어도 된다.

altitude.en: the height of something above sea level or the ground

ex.en: The plane cruised at an altitude of 10,000 meters.

aluminum.en: a light, silver-colored metal used to make cans, foil, and other products

ex.en: The can is made of aluminum.
Her talent for painting amazed everyone.
ex.ko: 그녀의 그림 재능은 모두를 놀라게 했다.

ex.en: She stared in amazement at the magic trick.

amazingly.en: in a way that is surprising or impressive

ex.en: Amazingly, no one was hurt in the accident.
ex.ko: 놀랍게도, 그 사고에서 다친 사람은 없었다.

ambassador.en: an official who represents their country in a foreign country

ex.en: She was appointed as the ambassador to France.

ambiguity.en: the quality of having more than one possible meaning, which causes confusion

ex.en: The contract's wording caused a lot of ambiguity.

ambiguous.en: having more than one possible meaning; not clear

ex.en: His answer was deliberately ambiguous.

ammonia.en: a strong-smelling gas or liquid used in cleaning products and industry

ex.en: The cleaner had a strong smell of ammonia.
ex.ko: 그 세제에서 암모니아 냄새가 강하게 났다.

amongst.en: surrounded by or included in a group of people or things (formal form of 'among')

ex.en: She felt at home amongst friends.
There was ample food for everyone at the party.
ex.ko: 파티에는 모두를 위한 충분한 음식이 있었다.

amplifier.en: an electronic device that makes sound or signals louder or stronger

ex.en: He connected the guitar to the amplifier.

amplify.en: make a sound, signal, or effect stronger or greater

ex.en: The speakers amplify the singer's voice.

amuse.en: make someone laugh or feel entertained

ex.en: The clown amused the children with his tricks.
ex.ko: 그 광대는 재주로 아이들을 즐겁게 해주었다.
